name: From Cartama to Pizarra by road – circular route
name_original: De Cartama a Pizarra por carretera – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cártama are characterized by the fertile Guadalhorce Valley, offering a mix of flat riverside paths and more challenging climbs into the surrounding hills. The region's diverse terrain includes extensive agricultural lands, particularly orchards, alongside the Guadalhorce River. Cyclists can expect mostly paved surfaces, with routes extending into the rugged Sierra de Los Espartales for higher elevations. Best road cycling routes around Cártama The most popular road cycling route is From Cartama to Pizarra by road – circular route, an easy 18.4 miles (29.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle ride through the scenic Guadalhorce Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Caminito del Rey – View of Canillas de Aceituno loop from Cártama, a difficult 61.6 miles (99.2 km) path. This route features challenging climbs and expansive views towards notable landmarks. Local road cyclists also love the View of Caminito del Rey – View of Álora Castle loop from Cártama, a 79.3 miles (127.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Cártama is defined by fertile valleys, the Guadalhorce River, and rugged hills, offering a blend of flat sections and ascents. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to difficult mountain passes. The routes in Cártama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 7000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cártama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Cártama?
A: Cártama's road cycling routes feature diverse terrain, from the fertile Guadalhorce Valley with its extensive agricultural lands and riverside paths, to the more rugged Sierra de Los Espartales offering challenging climbs and mountain passes. You'll find a blend of flat sections and ascents, with routes often featuring mostly paved surfaces.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cártama?
A: Yes, Cártama offers routes suitable for beginners. For an easy ride through the scenic Guadalhorce Valley, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939045324/from-cartama-to-pizarra-by-road-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cartama to Pizarra by road – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 18.4-mile (29.7 km) trail.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling climbs around Cártama?
A: For challenging climbs and significant elevation gain, the region around Cártama, particularly towards the Sierra de Los Espartales, offers demanding routes. A difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39555960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Caminito del Rey – View of Canillas de Aceituno loop from Cártama&lt;/a&gt;, a 61.6-mile (99.2 km) path with substantial ascents.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes in Cártama?
A: Many routes follow the picturesque Guadalhorce River, offering views of its riparian vegetation and the fertile agricultural lands of the Guadalhorce Valley. Higher elevation routes provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling trails in Cártama?
A: Yes, several routes offer stunning viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Caminito del Rey – View of Álora Castle loop from Cártama&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views towards notable landmarks and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/687073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore near Cártama's cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, Cártama is rich in history. You can find the remnants of the Moorish Castle in the Sierra de Cártama, offering panoramic views. The 16th-century Church of Saint Peter the Apostle and the Hermitage of Our Lady of Remedies are also significant historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/687073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora Castle&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark accessible via some routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Cártama?
A: While many routes involve some elevation, the easier paths along the Guadalhorce Valley, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939045324/from-cartama-to-pizarra-by-road-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cartama to Pizarra by road – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, are generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride on paved surfaces.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cártama?
A: Cártama benefits from a mild climate, making road cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. Even in winter, routes are often accessible, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Cártama?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cártama are designed as circular loops. An example is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939045324/from-cartama-to-pizarra-by-road-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cartama to Pizarra by road – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which brings you back to your starting point.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cártama?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the scenic valley paths to challenging mountain climbs, and the well-maintained paved surfaces that make for an excellent road cycling experience.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments like cafes or pubs along the routes?
A: Cártama and its surrounding villages, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6311037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pizarra&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora&lt;/a&gt;, offer various local establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. Many routes pass through or near these settlements, providing opportunities for a break.

Q: Is there public transport available for cyclists in Cártama?
A: Cártama is well-connected to Málaga, just 17 km away, and other charming Andalusian villages. While specific bike-friendly public transport options should be checked with local providers, its accessibility to urban centers suggests various transport links are available, potentially accommodating cyclists.
